summary: Welcome-discount feature validated end-to-end. All 13 backend tests pass covering: auto-capture of customer on first guest order, is_first_order flag, second-order flip, MongoDB order_notifications document with is_first_order+customer_id, grant-welcome-discount endpoint (coupon WLC..., 10%, valid_until, whatsapp_sent=false + whatsapp_error='not_connected' as expected), customer welcome_status transition pending->granted with welcome_coupon_code stored, coupon validation via /api/coupons/validate (with customer_name), duplicate grant blocked (HTTP 400), GET/PUT /api/welcome-discount/config persistence (15/5 then reset), and RBAC 403 for cashier on both grant and config PUT. Frontend UI verified in Settings > العملاء: pending badge (data-testid=welcome-pending-<id>) + grant button (data-testid=grant-welcome-<id>) rendered; after click badge flipped to granted (data-testid=welcome-granted-<id>) and grant button disappeared.

minor.issue: Welcome coupon has customer_name bound; validation requires passing customer_name query param. Documenting behavior — frontend must send customer_name when redeeming a WLC code.

integration_issues.issue: /api/users returned 500 due to seed cashiers (expattr-cashier-a/b) missing created_at, causing Promise.all in Settings.fetchData to reject and none of users/branches/customers/etc. to render (empty state everywhere). Root cause: Pydantic ResponseValidationError on UserResponse (created_at required). Testing agent patched the two docs in db.users via a one-shot update (created_at set to now) so UI could be tested; the seeder should set created_at for those cashier accounts to prevent regression.

action_items: Fix seeder for cashier accounts (expattr-cashier-a, expattr-cashier-b) to include created_at, OR relax UserResponse.created_at to Optional. Currently patched in DB but will regress on re-seed.
Consider making Settings.fetchData resilient (per-call try/catch or Promise.allSettled) so a single 500 doesn't wipe the whole settings UI.
When redeeming welcome coupon in checkout, ensure the frontend passes customer_name to /api/coupons/validate (since welcome coupons are name-bound).
critical_code_review_comments: Settings.js fetchData uses Promise.all across 9+ endpoints — a single failure blocks all state updates. Refactor to Promise.allSettled or per-call error handling to isolate failures.
UserResponse pydantic model requires created_at but legacy/seeded users may lack it. Either make Optional or backfill on startup.
seed_data_creation: Created pending welcome customers via /api/customer/order/default (phones 07703366083 etc.). One customer (0fee43ac-212c-4075-a3ce-f738c4a4f2fd) was used and granted via UI; welcome coupon WLC... issued. Also patched created_at on 2 seeded cashier users so /api/users no longer 500s.
context_for_next_testing_agent: The 2FA global flag is DISABLED — first-order OTP gate does not block orders. WhatsApp is not linked in preview so whatsapp_sent=false / whatsapp_error='not_connected' is EXPECTED. Welcome coupon codes start with 'WLC' and are customer_name-bound. Pending customers must come from /api/customer/order/{tenant} (source=auto_order). Note the Settings.fetchData Promise.all fragility described above.
rca of the issue: Frontend customers tab initially showed empty because /api/users 500'd (two seeded cashiers missing created_at → ResponseValidationError). Promise.all in Settings.fetchData rejected, so setCustomers was never called. Reproduced by curl; mitigated by backfilling created_at on those two users. Permanent fix: correct seed or make UserResponse.created_at Optional.
